title: Prometheus の 403 エラー：原因と解決策
reasons: The article accurately identifies the `--web.enable-admin-api` flag, firewall rules, and reverse proxy configurations as common causes for Prometheus 403 errors related to administrative APIs.
The provided code examples for enabling the admin API and configuring firewalls/Docker are practical and helpful.
The direct link to Prometheus's official Management API documentation is a strong primary source.

The initial statement regarding 403 errors not being due to insufficient authentication information or permissions is an oversimplification. While true for the disabled admin API, 403s can frequently arise from authentication/authorization failures in other Prometheus contexts, especially when scraping targets in secure environments like Kubernetes.

sources.title: Security model - Prometheus
sources.claim: Prometheus 403 error often relates to administrative APIs (such as delete operations and snapshot generation) and `--web.enable-admin-api` flag, which is disabled by default for security reasons. If enabled, administrative and mutating functionality will be accessible under the /api/*/admin/ paths. Launching Prometheus without this flag will result in 403 for admin endpoints.

sources.title: Prometheus Management API
sources.claim: Prometheus provides a management API for administrative tasks.

sources.title: Flags - Prometheus
sources.claim: Prometheus uses port 9090 for its UI, API, and telemetry by default, configurable via `--web.listen-address` flag.

sources.title: Troubleshoot Prometheus data source issues | Grafana documentation
sources.claim: A 403 Forbidden error in Prometheus can occur if a reverse proxy is blocking the request or if there are issues with user read access to the Prometheus API or security settings.

sources.title: EC2にprometheusとgrafanaを構築する #AWS - Qiita
sources.claim: In EC2 environments, security group inbound rules must be configured to allow access to Prometheus's default port (9090).

sources.title: Prometheus Port Configuration: A Detailed Guide | Last9
sources.claim: Prometheus uses port 9090 for its web interface and API by default. Misconfigured ports can lead to security vulnerabilities, data black holes, and troubleshooting nightmares.

sources.title: Prometheus Web API is not enabled - Stack Overflow
sources.claim: When running Prometheus with Docker Compose, the `--web.enable-admin-api` flag must be passed as a YAML list of values in the `command` property.

sources.title: Getting 403 on all POST requests when behind a reverse proxy - On-Premise - #sentry
sources.claim: A reverse proxy (like Nginx or Apache) can return a 403 Forbidden error if its ACL (Access Control List) settings block certain endpoints.

sources.title: Prometheusを利用する際のセキュリティモデル ~公式ドキュメントを読む~ - TECHSTEP
sources.claim: The `--web.enable-admin-api` flag controls access to administrative APIs in Prometheus (e.g., deleting time series) and is disabled by default for security reasons. If enabled, administrative and mutating functionality are accessible under `/api/*/admin/` paths.

sources.title: CentOS 8 : Prometheus : データを削除する - Server World
sources.claim: To use the data deletion API, the Admin API, which is disabled by default, must be enabled.

sources.title: prometheusを動かしてみる - SCRAWLED TECH BLOG
sources.claim: Firewall rules need to allow TCP traffic on port 9090 for Prometheus to be accessible.

sources.title: How To Fix "server returned HTTP status 403 Forbidden" in Prometheus - Henry Xie 's blog
sources.claim: Prometheus can return 'server returned HTTP status 403 Forbidden' errors.

improvement_suggestions: Optional improvement: add official or primary source URLs for unsupported or weakly sourced claims.

Optional improvement: Clarify the scope of the statement '認証情報の不足や権限不足ではなく' (not due to insufficient authentication information or permissions) in the 'エラーの概要' section. Explicitly state that this specific cause (disabled admin API) is distinct from potential authentication/authorization issues that might lead to 403s in other Prometheus interactions (e.g., scraping targets in secured environments).

Optional improvement: Add a note about checking authentication/authorization configurations (e.g., service account tokens, RBAC for Kubernetes targets) as an additional troubleshooting step for 403 errors that are not resolved by the listed causes, especially if the 403 occurs during metric scraping.
Optional improvement: Review current official docs and update changed API/spec/pricing details.
